original_reasoning: Arabic belly and unending eating connect clearly to Greek cow dung through the action-to-result chain of consumption and excretion.
epiboētheō=come to aid; b3th=send, dispatch (especially army). Strong semantic+phonetic chain b-3-th/b-o-ē-th.
Arabic 'tight skin-container' strongly meets Greek 'bottle gourd' by the container/vessel function.
Arabic 'show/reveal' aligns well with Greek 'interpretations/acceptations' as revealed or received meanings.
Clear chain through speech: Arabic has 'much talking'; Greek is 'you were spoken/said'.
Arabic خط can denote a tract or place on the ground, matching Greek earth as the concrete spatial base.
low/downwardness naturally yields stumble or false step; this is a clear semantic chain.
Arabic مسلاخ refers to sloughed skin or stripped outer layer; Greek names a rough-skinned cartilaginous fish, a clear concrete chain.
